manifest.author: Alexis Rondeau, Micha Brugger, Jason Qin
manifest.name: Footnote Shortcut

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Beta 7 — the linting update.

**What to test**
- The lint command (renamed from tidy; command id is now `lint-footnotes`, so re-bind your hotkey if you had one on Tidy footnotes)
- Settings: one **Linting** section now holds the three lint steps, the two reindex options, and the new triggers **Lint on save** / **Lint on focused file change** (both off by default)
- Your old tidy* settings values migrate automatically on first load — check your toggles carried over
- 22 bug fixes from the adversarial bug hunt, highlights: case-insensitive footnote ids (matches Obsidian; fixes silent data loss on reindex), CRLF notes no longer get their frontmatter rewritten, code fences inside callouts/blockquotes are protected, definitions are no longer moved into unclosed code fences, no more duplicated '# Footnotes' headings, reindex is idempotent
releases.name: 0.2.0-beta.7

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Bug-hunt hardening release — 22 confirmed bugs found by an adversarial test sweep and fixed, each pinned by a regression test. Nothing user-facing changed; this is a robustness pass over the footnote transforms and navigation.

**What to test (the areas most affected):**

- **Reindex / Tidy / Move-to-bottom** on messy notes — especially:
  - Notes with **CRLF line endings** (Windows/synced): frontmatter and line endings are now preserved instead of being rewritten.
  - Fenced code **inside callouts/blockquotes** (`> ` prefix) and **unclosed code fences** mid-note — definitions are no longer renumbered inside them or relocated into them.
  - Footnote definitions inside **`<!-- HTML comments -->`** — no longer extracted or deleted.
- **Footnote IDs are now case-insensitive** (matching Obsidian): `[^Note]` and `[^note]` are treated as the same footnote everywhere — reindex no longer deletes or mis-pairs a case-variant definition.
- **Navigation**: jumping from a definition that sits *above* its marker; double-pressing the named-footnote hotkey (no longer nests `[^[^]]`).
- **End-of-word insertion**: accented words (é, combining marks) and words ending in `!`/`?`.
- **Tables**: rows written without a leading pipe (`A | B`).

Full suite: 242 passing, plus 5 pinned "spec question" tests tracking open behavior decisions (numeric footnote-prefix under reindex, markers inside math/HTML-comments, etc.).

Install/update via BRAT (`MichaBrugger/obsidian-footnotes`). Please report anything that behaves unexpectedly.
releases.name: 0.2.0-beta.6 (bug-hunt hardening)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Beta for BRAT testing. Since beta.4: footnote navigation jumps (marker <-> detail, and the jump after creating a footnote with the popup off) now land the cursor centered in the viewport instead of at the screen edge — most noticeable on mobile, where the cursor previously ended up nearly off screen behind the toolbar.
releases.name: 0.2.0-beta.5

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Icon comparison build: same plugin as 0.2.0-beta.3, but with the revised action-style icon set — hash + down-arrow (numbered), text cursor + down-arrow (named), text cursor + up chevron (inline), clipboard + up chevron (paste inline). Install this and beta.3 alternately via BRAT to compare both icon families on device.
releases.name: 0.2.0-beta.4 (action-style icons)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Beta for BRAT testing. New since beta.2: two inline-footnote commands (^[] quick-write with second-press exit, and paste-from-clipboard with sanitized content), consecutive footnotes insert immediately after an existing marker instead of navigating (#49), rapid-press popup fixes (save ordering — no more lost footnotes or cursor jumps, with a saving notice when a press has to wait), popup latency work, and the hand-drawn MARKER-STYLE icon set for all four commands (on-device evaluation build; an action-style build follows as beta.4).
releases.name: 0.2.0-beta.3 (marker-style icons)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Beta for BRAT testing: table-cell footnote inserts now go through the cell's own editor (fixes table corruption), footnote details only recognized at line start, and a warning Notice for footnote names containing spaces. Full unit + smoke test suites added.
releases.name: 0.2.0-beta.2

releases.author: Comprehensive-Jason
releases.description:
# 🎉Big New Features!🎉

## New Footnote Autosuggester

Type repeated footnotes faster with the autosuggester!

![ezgif com-video-to-gif (1)](https://github.com/MichaBrugger/obsidian-footnotes/assets/68677082/f93f8828-f199-40a3-a9c3-0614bdb96e5b)

## New Footnote Section Heading

Automatically adds a customizable section heading separating your footnotes from the rest of your note, reducing clutter.
_Disabled by default, please enable and customize in plugin settings._

![ezgif com-video-to-gif](https://github.com/MichaBrugger/obsidian-footnotes/assets/68677082/6e53a654-eac0-4077-a2cf-fc76d5ef3961)

releases.author: Comprehensive-Jason
releases.description: - Restored compatibility with iOS by removing regex lookbehinds

releases.author: Comprehensive-Jason
releases.description:
## We added a ton of new features in this release:
- ability to add non-numeric (named) footnotes with a new hotkey. #7 
- new icons for commands on mobile toolbar #8 
- Fixed errant behavior when adding multiple references to one footnote #11 

## **Please make sure to look through the README again, some things have been updated. You may have to reassign your hotkeys for this plugin again.**
